domain:
A Person has a name and an identifier. Every Person is the child of another Person and can have an indeterminate number of children (however, there will be one Person who is not the child of any other Person). A Person has an indeterminate number of ancestors (the ancestors of a Person are their father and the ancestors of their father). We will be interested in knowing the degree of each ancestor of a Person (1 for the father, 2 for the father's father, etc.).

A Resource has a name. There are two types of Resources, such as Files and Folders. Every resource belongs to a Folder and cannot exist outside of a Folder (however, there will be one Folder that does not follow that rule, the root Folder does not belong to any other Folder). Each Folder contains an indeterminate number of Resources.

A User is a Person who, in addition to a name and an identifier, also has a password to access an indeterminate number of Resources, and each Resource can be accessed by an indeterminate number of Users. When a User can access a resource, we will be interested in knowing if they can view it and if they can edit it.

questions.question:
Is ancestor degree a relevant domain concept that should be included in the model?

For example:
1. Ancestor degree is a relevant concept in this domain and should be represented in the model.
2. Ancestor degree does not correspond to any relevant domain concept and should be removed from the model.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Keep ancestor degree, it is a relevant domain concept.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Remove ancestor degree, it is not relevant.
questions.selected: Option 1: Keep ancestor degree, it is a relevant domain concept.

questions.question:
Should ancestor degree be modeled as a separate class associated with Person, or as a simple representation within Person?

For example:
1. Ancestor degree is a separate class with attributes: ancestor and degree. This allows users to query and filter by individual ancestor degree properties.
2. Ancestor degree is modeled as a simple represntation in Person. This is suitable when ancestor degree details are not needed.
Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The AncestorDegree is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The ancestor degree is a simple representation in Person.
questions.selected: Option 1: The AncestorDegree is a separate class with its own attributes.

questions.question:
Is ancestor a relevant attribute of ancestor degree that should be included in the model?

For example:
1. Ancestor captures important domain information about ancestor degree.
2. Ancestor does not represent important domain information for ancestor degree.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Keep ancestor, it is relevant information for ancestor degree.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Remove ancestor, it is not a relevant information.
questions.selected: Option 1: Keep ancestor, it is relevant information for ancestor degree.

questions.question:
Is degree a relevant attribute of ancestor degree that should be included in the model?

For example:
1. Degree captures important domain information about ancestor degree.
2. Degree does not represent important domain information for ancestor degree.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Keep degree, it is relevant information for ancestor degree.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Remove degree, it is not a relevant information.
questions.selected: Option 1: Keep degree, it is relevant information for ancestor degree.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Person in the relationship with AncestorDegree?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an ancestor degree can exist without any associated person.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an ancestor degree must have at least one associated person.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, AncestorDegree can exist without Person.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,AncestorDegree must have at least one Person.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,AncestorDegree must have at least one Person.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of Person in the relationship with AncestorDegree?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, an ancestor degree is associated with exactly one person.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, an ancestor degree can be associated with multiple people.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each ancestor degree links to one person.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each ancestor degree can link to multiple people.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each ancestor degree can link to multiple people.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of AncestorDegree in the relationship with Person?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a person can exist without any associated ancestor degree.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a person must have at least one associated ancestor degree.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Person can exist without AncestorDegree.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Person must have at least one AncestorDegree.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Person must have at least one AncestorDegree.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of AncestorDegree in the relationship with Person?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a person is associated with exactly one ancestor degree.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a person can be associated with multiple ancestor degrees.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each person links to one ancestor degree.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each person can link to multiple ancestor degrees.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each person can link to multiple ancestor degrees.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of User in the relationship with Resource?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a resource can exist without any associated user.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a resource must have at least one associated user.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Resource can exist without User.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Resource must have at least one User.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Resource can exist without User.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of User in the relationship with Resource?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a resource is associated with exactly one user.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a resource can be associated with multiple users.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each resource links to one user.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each resource can link to multiple users.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each resource can link to multiple users.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 2: Many as maximum cardinality, each resource can link to multiple users.

This option aligns with the domain description, which states that each Resource can be accessed by an indeterminate number of Users, indicating that multiple Users can be associated with a single Resource.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Resource in the relationship with User?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a user can exist without any associated resource.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a user must have at least one associated resource.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, User can exist without Resource.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, User must have at least one Resource.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, User can exist without Resource.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of Resource in the relationship with User?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a user is associated with exactly one resource.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a user can be associated with multiple resources.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each user links to one resource.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each user can link to multiple resources.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each user can link to multiple resources.
